name: What happens if my contractor finds hidden problems once work starts?
headline: What happens if my contractor finds hidden problems once work starts?

mainEntity.name: What happens if my contractor finds hidden problems once work starts?
mainEntity.text: What happens if my contractor finds hidden problems once work starts?

acceptedAnswer.text: In older homes around Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, and Half Moon Bay, hidden issues are common. Even a simple-looking remodel can uncover problems once walls and floors are opened. Surprises often include: - Old electrical wiring that isn’t up to current code - Worn or undersized plumbing that needs replacing - Rotten framing or subfloor from past leaks - Termite damage or dry rot - Foundation cracks or uneven floors A good general contractor will talk with you upfront about these risks and build some contingency into the budget and schedule. For example, you might set aside an extra 10–20% of the project cost for unknowns. If your contractor finds a hidden problem, they should stop, explain what they found, show you photos, and present options and costs before moving ahead. This discussion should be handled with a written change order that you sign. The big mistake is assuming nothing will go wrong and spending your whole budget on finishes. It’s smarter to budget for both the “seen” and “unseen” parts of the job. That way, if a surprise shows up, you can deal with it without going into panic mode or having to cut corners on safety.
